info.title: Sorkos Login API
info.description:
Endpoint contract for client projects integrating with Sorkos Login. This system is for registered Sorkos projects only and is not intended for public self-service use.

Implementation status: /authorize validation, the provider-selection screen, first-party E-Mail login, authorization-code issuing, /token, and /logout exist. External OAuth providers are planned but not implemented yet.

tags.name: Authorization
tags.description: Client integration entry points. Client projects should start sign-in here, especially with /authorize.

tags.name: Browser Provider Flow
tags.description: Internal browser-facing provider steps used after /authorize. Client projects must not call or link to these endpoints directly.

get.description: Starts the authorization-code flow for a registered client project.

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: Public client identifier stored in auth_clients.client_id.

parameters.name: redirect_uri
parameters.description: Callback URL. It must exactly match a row in auth_client_redirect_uris.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Random value generated by the client project and validated by the client after redirect.

parameters.name: scope
parameters.description: Requested profile fields. Version 1 expects basic profile/email scopes only.

parameters.name: lang
parameters.description: Preferred UI language, constrained by the client settings.

parameters.name: prompt
parameters.description: Controls behavior when a central Sorkos Login session already exists. By default, Sorkos asks the user to continue as the current account. Use prompt=none to allow silent SSO and immediately redirect back with an authorization code.

200.description: Valid request and no central login session, showing the provider-selection page.

400.description: Invalid client, redirect URI, response type, or missing state.

get.summary: Internal browser step: show E-Mail login form
get.description: Shows the first-party E-Mail provider form after a valid /authorize request has been stored in the browser session. Client projects must not call or link to this endpoint directly; they should start sign-in at /authorize.

400.description: No pending authorization request or provider not enabled.

post.summary: Internal browser step: send E-Mail login code
post.description: Creates a short-lived six-digit login code and sends it with PHP mail() from login@sorkos.net. This endpoint belongs to the Sorkos Login browser flow after /authorize, not to the external client integration contract.

400.description: No pending authorization request or provider not enabled.

get.summary: Internal browser step: show E-Mail code form
get.description: Shows the six-digit code form for the current browser session's email login challenge. Client projects must not call or link to this endpoint directly; they should start sign-in at /authorize.

302.description: Redirect to /oauth/email if no email challenge exists.

post.summary: Internal browser step: verify E-Mail login code
post.description: Verifies a single-use six-digit login code, creates or updates the local user and email identity, creates the central Sorkos Login session, then continues the original /authorize request. This endpoint is for the Sorkos Login browser flow, not direct client integration.

302.description: Code accepted; redirects back through /authorize with the new central auth session.

200.description: Code entry form with a localized validation error.

get.description: Redirects the user to Google after validating the pending authorization request. Planned, not implemented yet.

302.description: Redirect to the external OAuth provider.

400.description: Error page if no valid pending authorization request exists.

get.description: Redirects the user to Discord after validating the pending authorization request. Planned, not implemented yet.

302.description: Redirect to the external OAuth provider.

400.description: Error page if no valid pending authorization request exists.

get.description: Receives the Google callback, creates or updates the Sorkos Login user, creates the central auth session, then continues the original authorization request. Planned, not implemented yet.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Provider state generated by Sorkos Login.

302.description: Redirect back to the original client redirect_uri with code and the original client state.

400.description: Error page if provider state validation or provider login fails.

get.description: Receives the Discord callback, creates or updates the Sorkos Login user, creates the central auth session, then continues the original authorization request. Planned, not implemented yet.

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Provider state generated by Sorkos Login.

302.description: Redirect back to the original client redirect_uri with code and the original client state.

400.description: Error page if provider state validation or provider login fails.

post.description: Exchanges a short-lived authorization code for normalized user information. This request is made server-side by the client project. The client secret authenticates the client; the authorization code identifies the user session that completed /authorize.

400.description: Invalid request, code, grant type, client credentials, or redirect URI.

get.description: Destroys the central Sorkos Login session. Client projects still handle their own local logout separately. If client_id and redirect_uri are supplied, redirect_uri must exactly match a registered URI for that client.

parameters.name: client_id
parameters.description: Client requesting a post-logout browser redirect.

parameters.name: redirect_uri
parameters.description: Post-logout return target. Must exactly match a registered URI for client_id.

200.description: Logout confirmation page when no valid return URI is supplied.

302.description: Redirect to the validated post-logout return URI.

code.description: The code returned to the client callback.

client_secret.description: The client secret for confidential server-side clients.

redirect_uri.description: The same redirect URI used in /authorize.

error_description.example: The authorization code is invalid or expired.
